draft.brief: create a immersive travel experience for my students. We want to see what london is like

structure.label: Cube
structure.description: A compact immersive room for focused demos, classrooms, and collaborative reviews.

draft.suggested_structure_reason: A cube-style room works well for guided classes and compact operator-led sessions where the group needs a focused front-facing teaching flow.

assessment.recommended_route: Yes, Igloo already supports this class of content/workflow. The main thing to check is that the exact file, link, or prepared asset behaves as expected. Treat this as a configuration-led workflow. Likely runtime path: Use the documented 360-degree content workflow for panoramic imagery or 360 video.; A likely supported route is the dedicated YouTube 360 workflow, or a hosted/browser-based YouTube 360 path, for readily available immersive video content. Likely content-prep path: Source or create the 360 imagery/video before loading it into Igloo.
top_dependencies: A compatible 360 image, 360 video, or hosted 360 content source that the chosen layer can access.
Operator review before client-facing commitment if the answer carries ambiguity or risk.
top_unknowns: The final 360 asset source and whether to use hosted or local media still need to be chosen.
The team still needs to decide whether hosted YouTube 360 or locally prepared media is the better operational choice.

evidence.title: Desktop UI - Building a Homescreen, part 1 | Igloo Documentation
evidence.excerpt: An Igloo Core Engine homescreen acts as a landing page for the immersive space and presents the user with commonly used shortcuts such as recent sessions, favourite content and integrated applications.

evidence.title: Triggers and Actions | Igloo Documentation
evidence.excerpt: The Triggers and Actions system allows users and integrators to create customisable experiences by defining events (Triggers) that initiate specific Actions.

evidence.title: Desktop UI - Exporting and Importing Sessions | Igloo Documentation
evidence.excerpt: These files can be then copied to other Igloo Immersive Media Players…

assessment.practical_summary: Yes, Igloo already supports this class of content/workflow. The main thing to check is that the exact file, link, or prepared asset behaves as expected.

selected_content.title: 4K 360° VR | Immersive Travel Experience - YouTube
notes: Use a local 360 master or the documented YouTube 360 route when suitable footage already exists.
Prefer 4K or higher for room-scale playback.
Igloo already supports YouTube 360 as a workflow class when the hosted content behaves as expected.
Confirm that the exact video is actually 4K+ and suitable for in-room playback.

recommendations.title: Extend the runtime
recommendations.detail: This draft only covers about 9 minutes. Add another supporting beat such as archival stills, a PDF panel, or a reference website to reach at least 10 minutes cleanly.

recommendations.title: Keep the lesson to a few strong teaching beats
recommendations.detail: Plan the room like a class: immersive opener, guided explanation, one interaction or reveal moment, then recap/reset. Too many states make the lesson feel messy rather than immersive.

use_case_alignment: Comparable Igloo use-case pattern: immersive classrooms and teaching spaces use repeatable sessions, virtual field trips, and operator-guided learning content.
demo_plan_notes: Start with one immersive hook that sets the scene in the first 60-90 seconds, then move into guided teaching beats.
Keep the teacher or operator flow to a small number of clear states so the lesson feels structured rather than exploratory by accident.
Use interaction sparingly: one reveal, vote, or branch moment is stronger than lots of small clicks in a classroom demo.
Client-facing immersive demos usually work best with one hero moment, two or three supporting beats, and one obvious reset path.
